response: The probability of exactly fifteen rooms needing attention is approximately point zero four three based on the Poisson distribution with a mean of twenty. For the second part the chance that at least seven of eight concerns are resolved successfully equals the sum of probabilities for seven successes and eight successes under a binomial model with a ninety percent success rate. This calculation yields a combined probability of roughly point one nine four. The concierge resolves most issues while the manager handles the rest of the workload for the one hundred rooms in the hotel.
